mappings.rationaleAI: Genetic determinism directly addresses twin studies, behavioral genetics, the search for genes linked to traits, and eugenics history -- covering inheritance of behavioral traits, fraternal and identical twin studies, and genetic explanations for abnormalities.
Genetics covers Mendel's laws, chromosome theory, DNA structure, and the Human Genome Project -- directly relevant to the biological basis of human heredity and applications of human genetics.

Human nature covers the nature-nurture debate, twin studies, behaviorism versus nativism, and gene-environment interaction in epigenetics -- directly relevant to inheritance of behavioral traits and specific behavioral traits affected by inheritance.

Evolutionary psychology covers evolutionary explanations for human behavior including sexual selection, the nature-nurture debate, and genetic determinism controversies -- relevant to inheritance of behavioral traits and specific behavioral traits.

Genetic mutation covers chromosomal rearrangements, mutations causing genetic diseases (sickle cell anemia, cystic fibrosis), and the relationship between mutation and disease -- directly relevant to genetic explanations for abnormalities through chromosome variations and mutation.
Neanderthal-Homo sapiens interbreeding (2-4% Neanderthal DNA in modern non-Africans) provides concrete evidence of human physical variation and its origins through ancient genetic admixture.
Mitochondrial genetics and maternal inheritance patterns represent a distinct mode of human hereditary transmission relevant to the biological basis of human heredity.
